situation: opponent casts seijiri shelter. can deflecting swat change its target to a creature I control?
ruling: Yes, you can use Deflecting Swat to change the target of Sejiri Shelter to a creature you control, provided that creature is a legal target for Sejiri Shelter (i.e., a creature you control, since Sejiri Shelter targets "target creature you control").
explanation:
• Deflecting Swat allows you to choose new targets for a target spell or ability — Sejiri Shelter is a spell on the stack and is a legal target for Deflecting Swat.
• When choosing new targets, each new target must be a legal target for the original spell — Sejiri Shelter reads "target creature you control," so the new target must also be a creature *you* control.
• Since you are now the one choosing the new target, "you control" refers to you (the Deflecting Swat controller) at the time of re-targeting, meaning you can redirect it to one of your own creatures.
• The protection granted by Sejiri Shelter will then apply to your creature instead of your opponent's creature.
rules_cited: 115.7a: When changing targets, each new target must be a legal target for the spell or ability; if any target can't be changed legally, none are changed.
115.7c: "Choose new targets" allows any number of targets to be changed (Deflecting Swat uses this wording).

situation: opponent casts seijiri shelter. can deflecting swat change its target to a creature I control?
ruling: Yes, you can use Deflecting Swat to change the target of Sejiri Shelter to a creature you control, provided that creature is a legal target for Sejiri Shelter (i.e., a creature you control, since Sejiri Shelter targets "target creature you control").
explanation:
• Deflecting Swat allows you to choose new targets for a target spell or ability — Sejiri Shelter is a spell on the stack and is a legal target for Deflecting Swat.
• When choosing new targets, each new target must be a legal target for the original spell — Sejiri Shelter reads "target creature you control," so the new target must also be a creature *you* control.
• Since you are now the one choosing the new target, "you control" refers to you (the Deflecting Swat controller) at the time of re-targeting, meaning you can redirect it to one of your own creatures.
• The protection granted by Sejiri Shelter will then apply to your creature instead of your opponent's creature.
rules_cited: 115.7a: When changing targets, each new target must be a legal target for the spell or ability; if any target can't be changed legally, none are changed.
115.7c: "Choose new targets" allows any number of targets to be changed (Deflecting Swat uses this wording).
reason: Deflecting swat doesn't change control of sejiri shelter, hence it can only change the target to another creature the controller of sejiri shelter controls.

situation: Opponent cast sejiri shelter on his creature. Can deflecting swat change shelter's target to a creature I control?
ruling: Yes, you can use Deflecting Swat to change the target of Sejiri Shelter to a creature you control, granting your creature protection from the color of your choice instead of your opponent's creature.
explanation:
• Sejiri Shelter targets a creature, making it a legal target for Deflecting Swat's "choose new targets" effect.
• Deflecting Swat allows you to choose **any** new legal targets for the targeted spell — it says "you may choose new targets," which follows the rule that all targets may be changed (rule 115.7a).
• A creature you control is a legal target for Sejiri Shelter (it just has to be "a creature you control" — but note: Sejiri Shelter says "target creature you control," meaning the new target must also be a creature controlled by the *original caster* (your opponent).
• ⚠️ **Important caveat**: Sejiri Shelter's oracle text reads "target creature you control" — the "you" refers to the spell's controller (your opponent). A creature *you* control is not a legal target for Sejiri Shelter, since you are not the spell's controller.
• Therefore, Deflecting Swat **cannot** legally redirect Sejiri Shelter to a creature you control — the only legal new targets are other creatures your opponent controls.
rules_cited: - Rule 115.7a: Each target can only be changed to another **legal** target; if a target can't be changed to a legal target, the original target is unchanged.
- Rule 115.7c: "Choose new targets" allows any number of targets to be changed.
